title: The last time I had it and lost it
subtitle: A journal prompt set for examining real financial loss events and what they actually meant.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 19 of the MONEY program, using the Week 3 prompts from the MONEY Journal. Use when the fear that money won't stay is running in the background and you want to look at where it came from.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 19 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 3 daily prompts of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you notice you're spending money as soon as it arrives, or when holding a full account produces anxiety rather than relief.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 19 of the MONEY program. The prompts look at the specific memory behind the loss-fear: the real event (or the inherited story about events) that installed the belief that keeping money is unsafe. Twenty minutes, no editing.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
Where do I expect good things to slip away once I have them?
Not just with money. With anything: relationships, health, work, good patches. Write what you notice about the expectation of impermanence in your life.
What have I maintained or grown over time that shows I can be trusted with abundance?
Write at least two things. They don't have to be financial. Anything you've held and not lost.
The last time I had it and lost it — what actually happened?
Write the specific event, as plainly as you can. Not the feeling about it — the facts of it. What came in. What went out. The real reason it went.
Was that loss inevitable? Or was there a decision, a circumstance, or a different version of events that could have held it?
Write what you actually know, not the story you've made it mean.
How can I anchor more safety in keeping what I create, starting now?
One practical thing: a savings instruction, a decision not to check the balance on bad days, a single structural change. Write it.
Adapted from Day 19 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 3 daily prompts of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
